description: s3Dgraphy nodes datamodel including node types with CIDOC-CRM mappings. v1.6.0 inaugurates a systematic CIDOC-CRM mapping revision: complete audit of all node types against CIDOC-CRM, CRMarchaeo, CRMinf, CRMdig, CRMsci, CRMgeo; replacement of legacy 'cidoc_s3d' pseudo-class names (invented strings without IRI or superclass declaration) with formal em:/s3d: IRIs declared as proper subclasses of CRM classes; introduction of CRMinf as primary (not alternative) for the paradata chain (ParadataNode, PropertyNode interpretive case, ExtractorNode, CombinerNode); introduction of PROV-O for AuthorNode to cover both human and AI agents; alignment with the Heritage Digital Twin Ontology (HDT-O) for containment / granularity properties; LocationNodeGroup (v1.5.3) adopted as the reference pattern (CIDOC-native composition via P2_has_type → E55 Type instances, with explicit extension_status and rationale). Companion deliverables: em.ttl (formal EM extension ontology, dereferenceable via w3id.org/em/) and hdto_extension.ttl (HDT-O granularity properties: contains_HDT, contains_SU, isPrimary). v1.5.4 added RSF (Reused Special Find — spolia) as a stratigraphic subtype, octagon with red border, family=real, is_series=false. Originating DP: DP-26 (spolia project, last DP before the EM 1.5 cut). v1.5.3 added LocationNodeGroup (E53 Place + E55 Type) for spatial / locational membership orthogonal to ActivityNodeGroup (intention) and EpochNode (time). v1.5.2 added family + is_series classification metadata on stratigraphic subtypes.

Node.description: Base class for all nodes in the graph with CIDOC mapping

StratigraphicNode.description: Base class for all stratigraphic units within the graph structure. Inherits from Node and provides additional functionality specific to stratigraphy.

US.label: US (or SU)
US.description: Stratigraphic Unit (SU) or negative stratigraphic unit. Represents a physical stratigraphic unit (positive or negative). It corresponds to an identifiable physical layer or interface in archaeological documentation.

mapping.alternative: E26 Physical Feature + A8 Stratigraphic Unit

USVs.label: USV/s
USVs.description: Structural Virtual Stratigraphic Unit (USV/s). Represents a virtual reconstruction of structural elements, such as walls, columns, or other architectural components that are no longer physically present but can be reconstructed based on archaeological evidence.

mapping.rationale: USVs is a HYPOTHESIZED stratigraphic unit, not physically observed. Multi-typed in OWL as both crminf:I4_Proposition_Set (propositional nature) and crmarchaeo:A8_Stratigraphic_Unit (stratigraphic role). The legacy 'A2 Stratigraphic Volume Unit' mapping was semantically wrong (A2 = physical volume). v1.6.0 corrects this via em_extension.

USVn.label: USV/n
USVn.description: Non-structural Virtual Stratigraphic Unit (USV/n). Represents a virtual reconstruction of non-structural elements, such as furniture, decorations, or other non-load bearing components that are no longer physically present but can be reconstructed based on archaeological evidence.

mapping.rationale: Same correction as USVs: virtual = propositional, not physical A2. See em_extension.

SF.label: Special Find
SF.description: Movable physical object whose ORIGINAL position is unknown (and sometimes whose pertinence to the site is uncertain). Distinct from in-situ artefacts — known to exist(ed) but displaced. Architectural artefacts, coins, fragments without findspot. Provenance / pertinence uncertainty is captured via paradata edges, not by class.

mapping.alternative: E19 Physical Object + crminf:I2_Belief (for uncertain site-pertinence)
mapping.rationale: E19 captures the movable physical-object nature. The 'displacement' semantic (no original-position info) is intrinsic to the EM concept and is captured by the em:DisplacedSpecialFind class; uncertain pertinence to the site is expressed via paradata edges to a crminf:I2_Belief at instance level.

VSF.label: Virtual Special Find
VSF.description: Hypothetical reconstruction of a fragmented Special Find. Virtual reassembly of fragments supported by paradata. Linked to the source fragments (DisplacedSpecialFind instances) via em:reconstructsFrom.

mapping.alternative: E89 Propositional Object + E19 Physical Object
mapping.rationale: E89 (propositional) captures the hypothetical nature; multi-typed with E19 (physical object) because the reconstructed result is referred to as if it were the physical artefact.

RSF.label: Reused Special Find
RSF.description: Re-used architectural or decorative element (spolia) in archaeological reconstructions. A physical object observed in situ but originally produced for a different context — e.g. a Roman column re-used in a medieval church wall. Originating Development Project: DP-26 (spolia, last DP before the EM 1.5 cut).

mapping.alternative: E19 Physical Object + A2 Stratigraphic Volume Unit + em:wasReusedFrom (links to original context)
mapping.rationale: Spolia has DUAL nature: an E19 physical object with its own production history AND now an A2 SU embedded in the current stratigraphy. The legacy 'E19 + E11 Modification' alternative captured only the modification-event side; v1.6.0 model spolia as multi-typed E19 + A2 with em:wasReusedFrom pointing to the original (lost) context.

serSU.label: US series
serSU.description: Series of Stratigraphic Units (SU). A pragmatic stratigraphic aggregation: one logical SU representing multiple physical SUs treated as one (e.g. a series of column bases not worth numbering individually). The series IS itself an SU, not just a collection of SUs.

mapping.alternative: A8 Stratigraphic Unit + P46_is_composed_of (links to component SUs)
mapping.rationale: A series is itself an SU (A8) that aggregates components via P46_is_composed_of. The legacy mapping 'A6 Group Declaration Event' was wrong — A6 is the EVENT of declaring a group, not the group itself.

serUSD.label: USD series
serUSD.description: Series of Documentary Stratigraphic Units (USD). Pragmatic aggregation of multiple DocumentaryVirtualSUs (USD) treated as one logical unit. Same series-as-SU pattern as serSU, applied to the documentary family.

mapping.rationale: Like serSU, but the aggregated components are DocumentaryVirtualSU. Same correction over the legacy A6 mapping.

serUSVn.label: USVn series
serUSVn.description: Series of non-structural Virtual Stratigraphic Units. Pragmatic aggregation of multiple NonStructuralVirtualSU (USVn) treated as one logical unit. Same series-as-SU pattern as serSU.

mapping.rationale: The series IS still a virtual SU (subclass of em:NonStructuralVirtualSU), aggregating its components via P46_is_composed_of. Legacy 'E78 Collection' alone treated the series as a pure collection rather than a typed SU; v1.6.0 keeps the SU nature.

serUSVs.label: USVs series
serUSVs.description: Series of Structural Virtual Stratigraphic Units. Pragmatic aggregation of multiple StructuralVirtualSU (USVs) treated as one logical unit.

mapping.rationale: Same series-as-SU pattern as serUSVn but for the structural virtual family.

USD.label: USD
USD.symbol: white round rectangle with orange border
USD.description: Documentary Stratigraphic Unit. Represents a conceptual stratigraphic unit inferred from historical or documentary sources rather than direct archaeological observation. A USD is the RESULT of a document (an SU posited because a document attests it), NOT a document itself.

mapping.alternative: crminf:I4_Proposition_Set + A8 Stratigraphic Unit (linked to E31 Document via em:derivedFromDocument)
mapping.rationale: A USD is an SU posited on the strength of documentary evidence, not a document. The legacy 'E31 Document' mapping confused the asserted entity with its supporting source. v1.6.0 corrects via em_extension: USD is a Virtual SU (propositional) evidenced by documents.

TSU.label: TSU
TSU.symbol: dotted white rectangle with red border
TSU.description: Transformation Unit. A family of actions modifying a surface or structure — the 'map of degradation' treated stratigraphically. Aggregates multiple A5 Stratigraphic Modification events via P46_is_composed_of.

mapping.rationale: TSU is the canonical 'transformation' / 'degradation' class — A5 in CRMarchaeo. The 'family of actions' aspect is expressed by composition (P46) rather than by a separate class.

UL.label: UL
UL.description: Working Unit. Traces of stone working on architectural surfaces — chisel marks, gradina, iccozza traces on Etruscan tomb walls, hammer impacts. UL is the FEATURE (the marks themselves); the producing event is a separate E12 Production linked via P108i_was_produced_by.

mapping.alternative: E25 Human-Made Feature + P108i_was_produced_by E12 Production
mapping.rationale: UL is the trace, not the act that produced it. v1.6.0 corrects the legacy 'E12 Production' (= the production event itself) to 'E25 Human-Made Feature' (= the observable trace). The production event is modelled separately and linked via P108i.

BR.label: continuity node
BR.description: End of life of a SU/USV. Marks the point in time when a stratigraphic unit ceases to exist (destruction, dismantling, deep transformation that no longer preserves identity).

mapping.alternative: E64 End of Existence + A5 Stratigraphic Modification (when the end-of-life is itself a stratigraphic modification event)
mapping.rationale: E64 End_of_Existence is the canonical CIDOC class for the cessation event. A5 is an alternative when the cessation is also itself a stratigraphic modification.

SE.label: stratigraphic event node
SE.description: An event that creates, modifies, or destroys a stratigraphic unit (e.g. an earthquake causing a plaster collapse, an act of demolition, the start of construction). Distinct from the resulting unit itself: an SE is the PROCESS, the SU is the result. Can be enriched by a 3D simulation (em:RepresentationModel) of the event. May be paired with its resulting unit to provide a temporal range bracketing the action.

mapping.alternative: E5 Event + A4 Stratigraphic Genesis (for formation) or A5 Stratigraphic Modification (for modification) or E64 End of Existence (for destruction)
mapping.rationale: SE spans formation (A4), modification (A5), and destruction (E64). The generic parent is E5 Event; specific CRMarchaeo subclasses A4/A5 are alternatives used when the event nature is precisely typed. Legacy mapping 'A4 Stratigraphic Genesis' was too narrow (formation only).

USN.label: Negative SU
USN.description: Negative Stratigraphic Unit — a lacuna, removal, or interface that marks the absence of matter rather than its presence (e.g., a pit cut, an erosion surface, a demolition void). Distinct from a plain StratigraphicUnit (which is positive by default), but equally observable on the ground — hence classified in the `real` family.

mapping.alternative: A3 Stratigraphic Interface + A5 Stratigraphic Modification (the producing removal event)
mapping.rationale: A USN is an INTERFACE (A3) produced by removal of matter, not a positive volumetric SU (A2). The legacy 'A8 Stratigraphic Unit (Negative)' was not a real CRMarchaeo class. The removal action is a separate A5 event linked via P108i_was_produced_by.

EpochNode.label: Epoch
EpochNode.description: Node representing a temporal epoch. It is a convenient grouping that allows attributing a series of actions to a temporal delta. Once the specific chronology data, expressed as start_time and end_time properties of individual SUs, is defined through dating elements, these epoch nodes are ignored where such specific data are available.

GroupNode.description: Node that represents a group of nodes. These groups can be of various types: see subclasses below.

ActivityNodeGroup.label: Activity Node Group
ActivityNodeGroup.description: Group node for activities. An activity is a logical group of actions that are kept together for a narrative purpose and information order (e.g., construction of a room of a building in year x, restoration activities of various parts of that room 20 years later).

ParadataNodeGroup.label: Paradata Node Group
ParadataNodeGroup.description: Group node for paradata. This group brings together all paradata related to a stratigraphic unit: typically named '[US_name]_PD' (ParaData).

TimeBranchNodeGroup.label: Time Branch Node Group
TimeBranchNodeGroup.description: An alternative timeline aggregating a set of mutually exclusive beliefs about a temporal interval. Two TimeBranch instances can be connected via em:contrastsWith (symmetric) to express alternative reconstructions of the same period. The Period nature (E4) anchors temporal identity; the alternative-set semantic is captured by contrastsWith.

mapping.rationale: E4 Period anchors the temporal identity. The 'alternative set of beliefs' aspect is NOT modeled as I2 Belief (everything in EM is propositional by design) but as a symmetric contrastsWith relation between TimeBranches. This avoids over-typing every aggregator as a Belief.

LocationNodeGroup.label: Location Node Group
LocationNodeGroup.description: Group node for spatial / locational membership of stratigraphic units and paradata. Orthogonal to ActivityNodeGroup (intention) and EpochNode (time): a Location answers the question 'what (named) place is this in?'. Three required kinds coexist on the same axis: 'toponym' (external/administrative identity, e.g. Pompei), 'study' (operational/procedural identity, e.g. Saggio4/A2) and 'functional' (interpretive/semantic identity, e.g. Casa del Fauno / Room 12). Propagation is additive: multiple memberships compose, none overrides — distinct from EpochNode which is substitutive (finest-grained wins). Membership is m:n via multiple is_in_location edges; the optional 'is_primary: true' attribute on one edge marks the membership rendered as a yEd group folder in em-graph. Locations are hierarchical: a LocationNodeGroup can itself be is_in_location of another LocationNodeGroup. A Location is identitary, not geometric: for coordinates / EPSG / shifts use GeoPositionNode (linked at the CIDOC level via P161).

mapping.alternative: E53 Place + E55 Type (kind classifier via P2_has_type)
mapping.rationale: No new s3d:Location class is introduced. A LocationNodeGroup is a pure CIDOC E53 Place; its three epistemic planes (toponym/study/functional) are expressed by classification with E55 Type instances (P2_has_type), which is the CIDOC-native pattern. CRMarchaeo does not introduce a Place-level container distinct from E53, so plain E53 is preferred.

kind.description: Epistemic plane of the location. 'toponym' = external/administrative identity (Pompei, Lazio, Italia); 'study' = operational/procedural identity (saggio, settore, quadrato, sondage); 'functional' = interpretive/semantic identity (basilica, room A, courtyard). The three coexist on the same node and may compose.

mapping.pattern: The E53 Place is classified via P2_has_type by exactly one of three reserved E55 Type instances. CIDOC-native composition; no new property URI is introduced.

toponym.rationale: Names the toponym epistemic plane. CIDOC-core has no reserved E55 Type instance for the administrative/external-identity discrimination of an archaeological place; CRMarchaeo does not introduce equivalent type categorisations either. Candidate primitive for a forthcoming CIDOC-S3D extension.

study.rationale: Names the study/operational plane (saggio, settore, quadrato, sondage). No CIDOC-core or CRMarchaeo equivalent — CRMarchaeo carries process semantics on the activity side but not as a Place-classifying type. Candidate primitive for a forthcoming CIDOC-S3D extension.

functional.rationale: Names the functional/interpretive plane (basilica, room A, courtyard). No CIDOC-core or CRMarchaeo equivalent. Candidate primitive for a forthcoming CIDOC-S3D extension.

propagation.description: Declarative rule for how location membership propagates to descendants. Location is additive (memberships compose; none overrides), distinct from EpochNode which is substitutive (finest-grained wins).

mapping.rationale: Propagation is a meta-schema rule (how a graph traversal should compose memberships), not a fact about a specific Place. It is therefore declared at the schema level and never lifted to RDF triples. Triplestore round-trip uses the schema definition itself, not per-instance triples.

ParadataNode.description: Base class for nodes representing the reasoning chain (paradata) supporting an assertion. Not a document — the documentation lives at the source end of the chain (DocumentNode reached via ExtractorNode → CombinerNode → Paradata).

mapping.alternative: crminf:I1_Argumentation + E31 Document (for the document that records the argument)
mapping.rationale: ParadataNode is the HEAD of an argumentation chain — semantically a crminf:I1_Argumentation, NOT a document (E31). The legacy E31 mapping conflated the argument with the documentation about it. v1.6.0 promotes CRMinf to primary for the paradata chain (ParadataNode, ExtractorNode, CombinerNode).

PropertyNode.label: Property
PropertyNode.description: Node representing a property (qualia) of another node. The CIDOC class is CONDITIONAL on the qualia type — looked up at serialization time in em_qualia_types.json: dimensional qualia (height/width/weight) → E54_Dimension, typology qualia (color/style) → E55_Type, material → E57_Material, interpretive qualia (aesthetic_value) → crminf:I4_Proposition_Set. PropertyNode itself is the structural shell (em:Qualia); the semantic class comes from the qualia type catalogue.

mapping.alternative: DEPENDS ON property_type — see em_qualia_types.json: physical_material.dimensional → E54_Dimension; physical_material.material → E57_Material; cultural_interpretive.semantic → crminf:I4_Proposition_Set; functional → E55_Type; etc.
mapping.rationale: PropertyNode is a generic shell — its specific CRM class depends on the qualia it expresses. The RDF exporter resolves this conditionally (em.ttl declares em:Qualia as E1 placeholder; the qualia-specific class is added at serialization).

DocumentNode.label: Document
DocumentNode.description: Node that represents a document or source. Attributes: data (dict): Additional metadata, such as 'url_type'.

ExtractorNode.label: Extractor
ExtractorNode.description: Node that represents the act of extracting information / adopting a belief FROM a single source (DocumentNode). The mediator between a source and an assertion: 'I adopted this belief because the source says so'. Distinct from CombinerNode which combines MULTIPLE extractions.

mapping.rationale: I7 Belief Adoption in CRMinf is the exact CRM-side counterpart of an extractor: the act of adopting a belief from a single piece of evidence. The legacy E7 Activity was too generic — it captured the activity but lost the epistemic nuance.

CombinerNode.label: Combiner
CombinerNode.description: Node that represents the act of combining MULTIPLE extracted beliefs into a single conclusion. The 'X and Y therefore Z' step in the EM paradata chain: combiner takes multiple ExtractorNode inputs and produces a synthesized assertion. Distinct from ExtractorNode (single-source belief adoption).

mapping.rationale: I5 Inference Making in CRMinf is exactly the act of producing a conclusion from premises — the CRM-side counterpart of EM's combiner. The legacy E7 was too generic.

GeoPositionNode.label: Geo Position
GeoPositionNode.description: Class to represent a GeoPosition node in the graph. Attributes: type (str): Node type, set to 'geo_position'. data (dict): Data related to geographic position.

LinkNode.label: Link
LinkNode.description: Class to represent a link node (LinkNode) in the graph. Attributes: url (str): URL of the link. url_type (str): Type of URL (e.g., 'External link', 'Image'). description (str): Description of the link.

mapping.alternative: E73 Information Object + E36 Visual Item

RepresentationModelNode.label: Representation Model
RepresentationModelNode.description: 3D representation (model or spatialized image) of a stratigraphic node — real or virtual. Always linked to the represented node via crm:P138_represents.

mapping.alternative: D1 Digital Object + SP6 Declarative Place + E52 Time-Span
mapping.rationale: D1 (Digital Object) from CRMdig is the canonical class for any digital asset. Multi-typed with SP6/E52 when the representation carries spatial and temporal anchoring.

RepresentationModelDocNode.label: Representation Model Document
RepresentationModelDocNode.description: 3D representation of a documentary node — e.g. a historical photo georeferenced and positioned in 3D space at the location it depicts. Linked via crm:P138_represents to a DocumentNode (or ExtractorNode/CombinerNode that uses the document). Specialization of em:RepresentationModel.

mapping.alternative: D1 Digital Object + P138 to DocumentNode
mapping.rationale: Subclass of em:RepresentationModel; the specific kind of target (document) is enforced structurally rather than as a flat property.

RepresentationModelSpecialFindNode.label: Representation Model Special Find
RepresentationModelSpecialFindNode.description: 3D representation of a Special Find — e.g. a scanned capital repositioned according to an anastylosis hypothesis. Linked via crm:P138_represents to a SpecialFindUnit / VirtualSpecialFind. Specialization of em:RepresentationModel.

mapping.alternative: D1 Digital Object + P138 to SpecialFindUnit / VirtualSpecialFind
mapping.rationale: Subclass of em:RepresentationModel; target type (special find) is enforced structurally.

SemanticShapeNode.label: Semantic Shape
SemanticShapeNode.description: 3D proxy geometry (convex shapes or spheres) representing the spatial extent of a node. Used as a semantic proxy distinct from a full RepresentationModel: lighter weight, intended for spatial indexing and rough containment, not for visual rendering. Linked via crm:P138_represents to any Node.

mapping.alternative: SP5 Geometric Place Expression + E36 Visual Item (legacy)
mapping.rationale: SP5 (CRMgeo) is the canonical class for geometric expressions of places — exactly what a proxy convex/sphere is. The legacy 'E36 Visual Item' was too generic (E36 is the appearance of any object). v1.6.0 promotes SP5 to primary.

AuthorNode.label: Author (human)
AuthorNode.description: Human author of one or more nodes. Identified by ORCID where possible. Python: AuthorNode (node_type='author'). The AI-assisted variant is AuthorAINode (subclass of AuthorNode). An isinstance check against AuthorNode matches both at the code level; the node_type discriminator and the em_extension.uri distinguish them semantically.

mapping.rationale: AuthorNode in Python is the canonical human-author class (node_type='author'). The em.ttl ontology declares em:Author as the abstract superclass with em:HumanAuthor / em:AIAuthor as siblings; the Python AuthorAINode inherits from AuthorNode purely for code reuse and isinstance() compatibility, not for OWL subsumption.

AuthorAINode.label: AI Author
AuthorAINode.description: AI agent (model + prompt_reference) responsible for asserting or refining one or more nodes. Python: AuthorAINode (node_type='author_ai'), subclass of AuthorNode. Already referenced as target in connections_datamodel.json has_author edge. The Python class carries 'model' and 'prompt_reference' in node.data for reproducibility.

mapping.rationale: AI agents are formally PROV-O SoftwareAgents; CIDOC E39 Actor is the parent on the CRM side. Reproducibility metadata (model identifier, prompt_reference) lives in node.data and is EM-specific — no native CRM equivalent.

LicenseNode.label: License
LicenseNode.description: Represents the license under which a node (or the whole graph via GraphNode) is published. Identified by license_type (e.g. 'CC-BY-4.0') and url to the license text. Connected via has_license edge to Nodes or GraphNode.

mapping.rationale: License is naturally an E30 Right in CIDOC-CRM (subject of P104_is_subject_to); dcterms:LicenseDocument provides Dublin Core anchoring for FAIR metadata interoperability. Both parents are kept to satisfy both CRM and Dublin Core consumers.

EmbargoNode.label: Embargo
EmbargoNode.description: Represents a temporal embargo on access or distribution of a node, license, or whole graph. Carries embargo_start, embargo_end (optional, indefinite if absent), and reason. Connected via has_embargo edge to a Node, LicenseNode, or GraphNode.

mapping.rationale: An embargo is a Right (E30) that restricts access for a bounded time period (linked via P4_has_time-span to E52 Time-Span built from embargo_start and embargo_end). Sibling to LicenseNode but with temporal restriction semantics.

GraphNode.label: Graph
GraphNode.description: Represents the EM graph itself as a node, used as anchor for graph-wide metadata (author, license, embargo, default epoch, project-level provenance). Enables graph-level inheritance for metadata propagation: values declared on the GraphNode become defaults for all contained nodes unless overridden. Targeted by has_author, has_license, has_embargo edges to carry global defaults. In HDT-O terms (D7.1) an EM graph IS a Heritage Proposition Set (HC16) — a named graph carrying formal assertions about a heritage entity. Connectable via contains_proposition_set (HP33) to one or more HDTNode (HC2 Heritage Digital Twin) instances.

mapping.rationale: GraphNode is conceptually three things simultaneously: an information artifact (E73), a bundle of triples (prov:Bundle), and a Heritage Proposition Set (hdto:HC16 from D7.1). Multi-typing captures all three perspectives — a CRM-only reader sees E73, a PROV-O reader sees Bundle, an HDT-O reader sees HC16 attachable to HC2 HDTs.

HDTNode.label: Heritage Digital Twin
HDTNode.description: Represents an HC2 Heritage Digital Twin (HDT-O, ECHOES D7.1). An HDT is a named container that aggregates Heritage Proposition Sets (em:EMGraph instances) about a heritage entity (HC1). HDTs can be nested hierarchically via has_digital_twin_component (HP3 inverse) — e.g. Colosseo_HDT_overall has component Colosseo_HDT_Flavian. An EM graph attaches to an HDT via contains_proposition_set (HP33). The HDT is linked to its real-world heritage entity (HC1) via has_digital_twin (HP1).

mapping.rationale: HC2 is the canonical HDT-O class for a Heritage Digital Twin. Per D7.1 §4.1: aggregate of named graphs carrying formal propositions about an HC1 Heritage Entity. Multi-typed via HC14 / D1 so consumers that don't know HDT-O still see a CRMdig Digital Object.

UnknownNode.label: Unknown node
UnknownNode.description: Fallback node for unrecognized types. This type of node should not normally appear in the system but serves exclusively to handle anomalies in the data.
